channel.title: briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: DHS expected to shut down as immigration talks falter
item.description: Senate Democrats blocked two Republican-backed measures Thursday to keep the department open, including a short-term funding extension for two weeks a...

item.title: ICE conducted 37 investigations into officer misconduct in last year
item.description: The disclosure from the agency's acting director came after immigration officers shot two U.S. citizens, intensifying questions about ICE officers' ta...

item.title: Trump Repeals Key Greenhouse Gas Finding, Erasing EPA’s Power to Fight Climate Change
item.description: The Environmental Protection Agency repealed the bedrock scientific finding that greenhouse gases threaten human life and well being. It means the age...

item.title: Ukrainians rally in support of Olympian Vladyslav Heraskevych, banned for his helmet
item.description: Ukrainians are uniting in solidarity with Olympic skeleton racer Vladyslav Heraskevych after he was disqualified for wearing a helmet with images of U...

item.title: Trump’s Actions Test the Fragile World of Air Travel
item.description: Last-minute announcements and abrupt changes by the Trump administration have caused confusion in an already strained U.S. aviation system.

item.title: El Paso Incident Highlights Gaps in America’s Drone Defense Industry
item.description: The U.S. has spent billions of dollars developing counter-drone technology, but much of it needs more testing in the real world.

item.title: Do Drug Cartels Actually Use Drones at the Border?
item.description: U.S. officials warn that cartel-operated drones on the border pose a major threat. Mexican officials are less certain. Analysts say the answer is like...

item.title: Winter Olympic shocker: American Chloe Kim falls short of halfpipe gold three-peat
item.description: Korea's Gaon Choi, 17, rebounded from a hard fall to win gold — and end her role model's historic bid for three in a row in the Winter Olympic halfpip...

item.title: California Investigates Why Most of the Eaton Fire Victims Were Black
item.description: The state’s attorney general opened an investigation into whether emergency responders failed to adequately warn the residents of a Black middle-class...

item.title: What the data tells us about kidnapped people — and how Nancy Guthrie is an outlier
item.description: Nancy Guthrie is among the thousands of people who go missing in the U.S. each year. But experts describe her case as "strange," with many unique deta...

item.title: Trump’s Actions Test the Fragile World of Air Travel
item.description: Last-minute announcements and abrupt changes by the Trump administration have caused confusion in an already strained U.S. aviation system.

item.title: Ontario Lifts Tuition Freeze at Public Universities
item.description: The move will allow the schools to raise tuition for the first time since 2019 as part of a broader plan to boost funding for higher education in the ...

item.title: Do Drug Cartels Actually Use Drones at the Border?
item.description: U.S. officials warn that cartel-operated drones on the border pose a major threat. Mexican officials are less certain. Analysts say the answer is like...

item.title: How Europe Woke Up to Trump
item.description: European leaders are wondering if they can ever trust the U.S. again.

item.title: Chloe Kim, once a teenage phenom, loses to a new one.
item.description: Kim was upset in heavy snow by a new star, 17-year-old, Choi Gaon of South Korea, in the women’s halfpipe.
